steps.target: to be unwilling / to not wish
choices: to be unwilling / to not wish

hints.to deny: That would be the infinitive of 'negare'
hints.to refuse: That would be closer to 'recusare'; 'nolle' means 'to be unwilling'
hints.to forbid: That would be the infinitive of 'vetare'

hints.present indicative: The present indicative would be 'non vultis'

hints.volo, velle, volui, —: These are the principal parts of 'volo' (to wish/want)
hints.malo, malle, malui, —: These are the principal parts of 'malo' (to prefer)
hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the principal parts of 'possum' (to be able)

hints.to be: That would be the infinitive of 'esse'
hints.to stay: That would be the infinitive of 'manere'
hints.to place: That would be the infinitive of 'ponere'

hints.sisto, sistere, stiti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'sisto' (to cause to stand)
hints.sum, esse, fui, futurus: These are the principal parts of 'sum' (to be)
hints.maneo, manere, mansi, mansum: These are the principal parts of 'maneo' (to remain)

hints.to know: That would be the infinitive of 'scire'
hints.to watch: That would be the infinitive of 'spectare'
hints.to perceive: That would be the infinitive of 'percipere'

hints.specto, spectare, spectavi, spectatum: These are the principal parts of 'specto' (to watch)
hints.cerno, cernere, crevi, cretum: These are the principal parts of 'cerno' (to discern)
hints.aspicio, aspicere, aspexi, aspectum: These are the principal parts of 'aspicio' (to look at)

steps.reveal: magnalia = great deeds / mighty works (acc. pl.)

hints.nominative: 'Magnalia' could be nominative plural neuter, but here it is the object of 'videte'

translation: Do not be afraid; stand and see the mighty works of the Lord
